text: A 55-year-old male presents with acute onset of severe, tearing chest pain radiating to the back. Most likely diagnosis?
rationale: Tearing pain to the back is classic for Aortic Dissection.

text: A 3-week-old infant has projectile non-bilious vomiting and olive-shaped mass. Diagnosis?
rationale: Pyloric Stenosis is the classic diagnosis for these findings.
